fig: noun. The fruit of the fig tree, pear -shaped and containing many small seeds.
fin: noun. One of the appendages of a fish, used to propel itself and to manoeuvre / maneuver.
fist: noun. A hand with the fingers clenched or curled inward.
fug: noun. A heavy, musty, stuffy or unpleasant atmosphere, usually in a poorly-ventilated area.
fungi: noun. Spongy, abnormal growth, as granulation tissue formed in a wound.
funs: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of fun
fusing: verb. present participle and gerund of fuse
gift: noun. Something given to another voluntarily, without charge.
gin: noun. A colourless non - aged alcoholic liquor made by distilling fermented grains such as barley, corn, oats or rye with juniper berries;
gist: noun. The main idea or substance, or the most essential part, of a longer or more complicated matter; the crux, the heart, the pith.
git: noun. A silly, incompetent, stupid, or annoying person (usually a man).
gnu: noun. A large antelope of the genus Connochaetes, native to Africa, having curved horns.
gun: noun. A device for shooting projectiles or ditto (a shooter), functioning through stored energy: a firearm, cannon, harpoon gun / spear
gust: noun. A strong, abrupt rush of wind.
gut: noun. The alimentary canal, especially the intestine.
nit: noun. The egg of a louse.
nut: noun. Any of various hard-shelled seeds or hard, dry fruits from various families of plants.
sign: noun. A visible fact that shows that something exists or may happen.
sin: noun. A violation of divine will or religious law.
sing: verb. To produce musical or harmonious sounds with one’s voice.
sit: verb. To be in a position in which the upper body is upright and supported by the buttocks.
sniff: verb. To make a short, audible inhalation, through the nose, as when smelling something.
snit: noun. A temper; a bad mood.
snuff: noun. Finely ground or pulverized tobacco (or other plant derivative) intended for use by being sniffed or snorted into the nose.
stiff: adjective. Rigid; hard to bend; inflexible.
sting: noun. A bump left on the skin after having been stung.
stuff: noun. Miscellaneous items or objects; (with possessive) personal effects.
stuffing: verb. present participle and gerund of stuff
stun: verb. To incapacitate; especially by inducing disorientation or unconsciousness.
stung: verb. simple past and past participle of sting
suing: verb. present participle and gerund of sue
suint: noun. A substance obtained from the dried perspiration of sheep wool, it is of a fatty and earthy consistency and contains potassium salts.
suit: noun. A set of clothes to be worn together, now especially a man's matching jacket and trousers (also business suit or lounge suit), or a
sun: noun. A star, especially when seen as the centre of any single solar system.
tiff: noun. A (generally small) argument or disagreement; a petty quarrel, a spat.
tin: noun. A malleable, ductile, metallic element, resistant to corrosion, with atomic number 50 and symbol Sn.
ting: interjection. Representing a high-pitched sharp sound like a small bell being struck.
tuff: noun. A light porous rock, now especially a rock composed of compacted volcanic ash varying in size from fine sand to coarse gravel.
tug: verb. To pull or drag with great effort.
tui: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tupuri.
tun: noun. A large cask; an oblong vessel bulging in the middle, like a pipe or puncheon, and girt with hoops; a wine cask. (See a diagram
tung: noun. Obsolete spelling of tongue, both as language and as part of the body.
unfit: adjective. Not fit; not having the correct requirements.
unfits: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of unfit
unit: noun. Oneness, singularity, seen as a component of a whole number; a magnitude of one.
using: verb. present participle and gerund of use
fugit: noun. the optimal date to exercise an American option (or a Bermudan option)
fung: noun. Alternative form of fum (“fabulous Chinese bird”).
funis: noun. A cord or a cord-like structure.
funt: noun. An old Russian unit of weight, approximately 410 grams.
fust: noun. A strong musty smell; mustiness.
fustin: noun. A yellow dye obtained from fustic, Venetian sumach (Cotinus coggygria).
fusting: verb. present participle and gerund of fust
gif: noun. Alternative letter-case form of GIF.
ing: noun. A meadow, especially a low meadow near a river; water meadow.
isn: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Isanzu.
ist: noun. A practitioner or supporter of an ism.
nis: noun. A nix; a hobgoblin, especially one that resides in a farm house.
nuff: adverb. Pronunciation spelling of 'nough, shortening of enough,
situ: noun. Only used in in situ.
snig: verb. To drag a log along the ground by means of a chain fastened at one end.
sugi: noun. Wood of the Japanese cedar (Cryptomeria japonica).
suni: noun. A small antelope of Southern Africa, Nesotragus moschatus.
tgn: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tandaganon.
tig: verb. To touch lightly or playfully; to trifle, dally.
tsun: noun. Dated form of cun (“Chinese unit of length”).
